title: Full Development Process (Nested Steps)
expectation: Use a worker-hosted feature-branch worktree as the only mutable implementation surface for active development in this process; never edit from the shared repository checkout, a manager-hosted worktree, or a worker shared checkout. Create that worker-hosted worktree from the current upstream base branch tip. Do not reuse an existing feature branch or worktree unless you created it earlier in the current process and have already verified that it is the intended mutable surface. After creating that worker-hosted worktree, merge only any additional upstream branch that is intentionally required beyond the chosen base branch before continuing. Connect through a persistent SSH session into the worker instance for the active development loop. Before implementation, explicitly read the relevant blueprint, update it if needed, read the relevant blueprint-state, and update it if needed. After each bounded implementation pass, update the relevant blueprint-state, ask the fixed team profile reviewer set to verify the blueprint-state against the blueprint and current implementation, and loop back into implementation whenever the team finds the blueprint-state incorrect or identifies remaining implementation work. Follow the full development-process blueprint through worker-backed blueprint updates, worker readiness verification with `verify-worker-surface`, committed implementation, worker-owned merge into `development`, worker-owned promotion onto `main`, worker-owned release-tag creation from the exact promoted commit, manager runtime checkout/update plus restart, runtime revision and version verification, live health verification, issuance of a manager-dashboard session URL with `bun run issue:dashboard-session`, real browser verification at the public Cloudflare manager dashboard URL using that issued session URL, manager-dashboard screenshot verification, cleanup of the feature branch and worktree on success, and rollback recovery when screenshot verification fails.
idlePrompt: If you fully completed the development process, reply only with 'done: full development process complete'. If you are truly blocked on a user or environment dependency, reply only with 'blocked: full development process blocked'. Otherwise resume the work immediately and perform the next required implementation, verification, release, deploy, or validation step instead of giving only a status update.
stopConditions: blocked on user or environment dependency

steps.title: Prepare the worker development surface
Complete the worker revision
Read the relevant blueprint
Update the relevant blueprint if implementation intent or ideal structure changed
Read the relevant blueprint-state
Update the relevant blueprint-state if the recorded current reality, current files, or planned files are stale before implementation
Confirm the current bounded implementation scope from the blueprint and blueprint-state
Implement the current revision on the worker branch
Make the next bounded code change on the worker branch
Run `bun lint` after each stable code change unless an explicit hotfix exception is recorded
Run `bun build` after each stable code change before that change can be treated as stable
Revise the current change until the required stable-change verification passes
Update the relevant blueprint-state to reflect the latest implementation reality
Ask each team profile sub agent to review the updated blueprint-state against the blueprint and current implementation and report correctness plus any remaining required changes
Is the blueprint-state correct and does the team agree that no more implementation changes are required for the current scope?

decision.prompt: Is the blueprint-state correct and does the team agree that no more implementation changes are required for the current scope?

options.title: The blueprint-state is incorrect, incomplete, or shows more implementation changes are required
The blueprint-state is correct and the current implementation scope is complete

steps.title: Commit the stable milestone on the feature branch

On the worker, use trusted `git` and `gh` authority to merge the finished feature branch into `development`, push `development`, merge `development` into `main`, push `main`, and create and push the release tag
What is the worker repository promotion outcome?

decision.prompt: What is the worker repository promotion outcome?

options.title: Worker repository promotion requires worker-side resolution

steps.title: Release, deploy, and validate the runtime
Clean up the feature branch and worktree
Summarize changes
